observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Acting MENRO to coordinate with the DENR-EMB Quezon City to facilitate the transfer of documents relative to the equipment received by the LGU and the necessary paper works to be prepared; MOA, Deed of Donation, Property Transfer as basis for recording in the books of accounts of the LGU by the Municipal Accountant and in the RPCPPE by the General Services Officer.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to draw a journal entry voucher to record in the books the Amended NFD to comply with Section 22.6 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 to fairly present the affected accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the:
Municipal Accountant to immediately prepare correcting/adjusting entries for discrepancies/errors or other reconciling items requiring adjustment in order to reflect the correct balance of the cash account in the financial statements; and
Municipal Treasurer to submit the disbursement vouchers & supporting documents for the unrecorded checks to the accounting office for recording of the transactions and the MA to submit these to the COA for post audit.

We recommended that Management:
Provide a stock room for proper storage of items of inventories or if no storage space is identified, require the end-user units to maintain a record of deliveries and issuances and submit the same to the GSO through the weekly preparation of SSMIs supported by the RIS. The SSMIs will then be consolidated by the GSO and submitted to the MA for recording of the actual issuances in the books of accounts and will help to determine the inventory balances as of a given date;
Require the Inventory Committee to conduct semestral physical inventory of supplies and materials and submit the Physical Inventory Report to the Auditor’s Office as required in Section 124, Volume 1 of the MNGAs; and
Direct the MA to strictly comply with Section 114, Volume 1 of NGAS Manual for LGUs for proper valuation of inventories and expenses presented in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to transfer funds from the TF and the SEF to the GF after the month when the remittance of taxes was made by the GF for fair presentation of the cash balance in the Financial Statements.

We recommended the following:
The MT shall submit the duplicate copy of the printed ORs;
The MT shall present to the Audit Team an accounting of the 129 missing official receipts. If these were unissued, present the unissued copies to the Audit Team. However, if these were already issued, submit the ORs and the required reports to the Municipal Accountant for proper recording in the books; and
The Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the MT to observe the prescribed rules and regulations for all collections and remittances.

We reiterated our recommendations that:
The Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the MT to observe the prescribed rules and regulations for all collections and remittances and
The MT shall strictly enjoin all RCOs to remit all collections intact daily to the MT, through the LO, and prepare the corresponding RCD on a daily basis. In case of manual collections made during non-working days (e.g. in markets, slaughterhouses), the collections shall be remitted immediately on the succeeding working day;
The LO shall strictly perform the verification of the accountable forms as to initial balances receipts, issuances, and ending balances on hand and perform a physical count of the remaining balance of the accountable forms in the custody of the RCOs; and
The MTO should require the RCOs to explain in writing the reason for committing consistent delays in remitting collections and institute appropriate sanctions such as relief from duties and filing appropriate administrative actions provided by applicable laws, rules and regulations, if warranted.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the MT to require the RCOs to immediately renew their fidelity bond in accordance with Section 101, Chapter 5 of P.D. 1445.

We recommended that the LCE shall direct the MPDO, Municipal Engineer, SWM Designated Officer and other concerned departments to:
Fast track the implementation of the PPAs funded by the 20% DF which are continued in CY 2024, particularly the SWM, so that the intended socio-economic, environmental and health benefits that can be derived therefrom can be attained; and
Monitor closely and ensure the efficient implementation of all priority development projects and programs under the 20% DF targeted to attain social, economic and environmental development in LGU – Virac.
Prospectively, the Management shall formulate a comprehensive 20% DF plan in which the accomplishment thereof is achievable during the budget year. They shall ensure that the development projects to be funded out of the 20% DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready as contemplated under Section 3.2.3 of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1.

We recommended that the LCE must secure a Sanggunian Bayan resolution ratifying the action of the former for entering into a contract as provided under Section 22 of R.A. No. 7160.
Should similar circumstances arise in the future, it's imperative that all concerned parties, including local chief executives and the Sangguniang Bayan, adhere to the provisions of Section 22 of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Engineer to submit a written justification for the inconsistency noted on the computation of mobilization/demobilization cost with the provisions of DPWH DO No. 197 series of 2016 dated October 7, 2016;
Should after the Audit Team’s evaluation the justification is untenable, require the contractor to refund the excess amount paid or deduct from remaining claim/s as necessary; and
Direct the Municipal Engineer to ensure the reasonableness of the computation of the mobilization/demobilization costs for all infrastructure projects by adhering to the provisions of applicable laws, rules and regulations to avoid excessive computation of the ABC.

We recommended that Management:
Demand the contractor to refund the cost of undelivered work items amounting to ₱32,648.31;
Require the contractor to rectify and correct the defects noted considering that it is still within the defects-liability period; and
Direct the Municipal Inspectorate Team to be cautious at all times in the conduct of inspection of LGU projects to ensure that projects are fully accomplished and defects are reported and recommended for rectification to ensure compliance with the approved plans and specifications indicated in the contract.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to comply with the provisions of Section 305 (a) of RA 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code of 1991, which provides that no money shall be disbursed unless there is still an existing appropriation that has been legally made and there is an available fund for the purpose.
Likewise, to ensure that LGU’s funds are allocated and spent based on the actual needs and priorities, agency officials should be guided by the principle of Section 306(f) which provides that Current Operating Expenditures refers to the appropriations for the purchase of goods and services intended for the conduct of normal local government operations within the fiscal year, and not for the operations of other agencies.

Require the General Services Officer to submit regularly to the Audit Team the consolidated Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Official Travels for audit and verification purposes.

materials semestrally and submit the Physical Inventory Report to the AT

timelines in the conduct of the procurement activities of goods and services, and

to end-users and immediate implementation of infrastructure projects for the benefit

prescribed by the same JC.

reports to concerned oversight department/agency within the period prescribed in
DepEd, DBM and DILG JC No. 2017-01.

we recommended that Management continue the good work and to monitor closely
the implementation of all DRRMF PPAs to attain 100 per cent accomplishments.

direct the Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) and the MA to desist from paying the operating expenditures of the PNP in compliance with Section 305 (a) of RA 7160; and

Likewise, to ensure that LGU’s funds are allocated and spent based on the actual needs and priorities, agency officials shall be guided by Section 306(f) which states that Current Operating Expenditures refers to appropriations for purchase of goods and services intended for conduct of normal local government operations within the fiscal year, and not for operations of other government agencies.

The deferment of future disbursement charged to 20% DF for projects incorporated in the current and continuing appropriations which are not classified as developmental projects under the subject JMC pending justifications thereof to ensure that funds are disbursed only for its intended purpose. observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to:
Coordinate with each other and reconcile their records with regards to the unrecorded credits memos to facilitate recording;
Record immediately in the books various credit memos with total balance of ₱3,206,827.37 as of December 31, 2018; and
Explain in writing their inability to record the credit memos in the books of the LGU.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the difference in their books, and thereafter make the appropriate adjustments and reclassification of accounts. Ensure that the concerned officers conduct regular reconciliation of records to arrive at the same correct balances in their respective records in consonance with Section 181(c) of GAAM, Volume I.

We recommended that the LCE:
instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and issue a demand letter to EBMC for the immediate liquidation of the CY 2018 financial assistance; and
instruct the Municipal Accountant to monitor strictly the timeline of the submission of the liquidation/fund utilization report for timely financial reporting.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant continue to conduct reconciliation of balances of Liability accounts against relevant records and effect proper adjustments for deficiencies noted and errors uncovered to reflect the correct account balances.
For those accounts under Due to NGAs, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
confirm whether the projects charged to particular trust funds were actually completed and identify the source agencies. If projects were already completed, return the identified account balances to the source NGAs; and
if source cannot be identified, remit immediately the remaining idle and unutilized funds to the Bureau of Treasury.

We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Officer, Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Development Council ensure that the projects funded from the 20% DF are development projects classified under Sections 2.3 and 3.0 of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1, and will contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcome for the locality.
We also recommended deferment of future disbursement charged to 20% DF for projects incorporated in the current and continuing appropriations which are classified as developmental projects under the subject Joint Circular pending justifications thereof to ensure that funds are disbursed only for its intended purpose.  Henceforth, financial transactions should be scrutinized carefully to ensure that only items that are directly associated in the implementation of developmental projects are properly charged to the 20% DF to maximize its utilization for more beneficial purpose.

We recommended that Management submit program of works, municipal resolutions, Local Development Council resolutions and the revised Annual Investment Plan to analyze further the appropriated projects vis-à-vis actual expenditures.  We also recommended that for lump sum appropriation, authorization from the Sangguniang Bayan should be obtained.

We recommended that the LCE, Municipal Planning and Development Officer, Municipal Budget Officer explain why the transfers of appropriations were not covered by an appropriation ordinance covering a supplemental budget.
We also recommended that Management ensure that future transfer of funds which are considered as realignment be done by way of appropriation ordinance covering a supplemental budget and be transmitted to the Sangguniang Panlalawigan for review.
We recommended that Management appropriate separate fund based on proposed budget intended for the implementation of SWM programs, projects and activities as required under Section 12 of RA No. 9003.

We recommended that the LCE consider making a study on the overall organizational structure of the LGU with emphasis on the staffing patterns to correct the wide salary gaps among employees for better delivery of public service.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Conduct reconciliation of trust accounts with the journals, ledgers and source documents.  Afterwards, make representation with the concerned government agencies with regards to the discrepancies noted in the books of the LGU, and adjust accordingly;
Remit immediately the determined unpaid obligations to the concerned government agencies to avoid incurrence of penalties and surcharges for late or non-remittance; and
Henceforth, conduct periodic reconciliation of balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Planning Officer prepare a comprehensive 20% Development Plan in which the accomplishment thereof is achievable during the budget year.
The LCE should also require the MPDO to submit monitoring report on the status of 20% Development Fund implementation, and use the same as basis in making decision in accordance with approved timetable/schedule embodied in the Annual Budget.
The implementation of the CY 2018 20% Development Projects must be fast tracked so that benefits therefrom could be realized in the earliest possible time.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Treasurer to:
Coordinate with the Municipal Assessor on the updated data and information of real Property Taxes for proper implementation of prescribed remedies and actions to generate more collections; and
Prepare and submit regularly a certified list of all real property tax delinquencies to the Sanggunian for the body to assist in the enforcement of the remedies for collection.

We commended Management for the substantial implementation of programs and projects funded by transfers from NGAs, thus, ensuring that the intended benefits were enjoyed by the beneficiaries of the municipality.  However, we also recommended that Management should strive for total implementation of projects by ensuring that (a) pre-engineering works such as identification of definite project sites should be done and integrated in project proposals prior to submission to the concerned NGAs for consideration; and (b) follow-ups of early releases of project funds be done in accordance with the approved timelines in the implementation thereof.

We recommended that Management consider appropriating separate fund intended for the procurement of mobile water purifying machine, instead of renting water tank from the PNRC which might be unreliable in future needs, to provide immediate response to affected constituents in times of calamities as provided under Section 12 (16) of RA No. 10121.

We commended Management and the Local School Board for the substantial implementation of PPAs funded by the Special Education Fund, thus, ensuring that the intended benefits were derived therefrom and enjoyed by the school populace.  However, we also recommend that Management strive for total implementation of the PPAs as embodied in the Annual School Board Budget.

We recommended the following:
The LCE shall require the Municipal Planning and Development Office to submit monitoring report on the status of POP implementation, and use the same as basis in making decision in project implementation in accordance with the approved timetable/schedule embodied in the annual budget.
The implementation of the Peace and Order Programs should be fast tracked so that benefits therefrom could be realized in the earliest possible time.

We recommended the following: a.  The Local Chief Executive create an Inventory Committee and require the same to conduct physical inventory and render report thereon as required under Section 124, Chapter 7, Volume I of the NGAS Manual. The progress of the inventory taking should be monitored by the Local Chief Executive. b.  Thereafter, the Committee, the General Services Office and the Municipal Accounting Office reconcile all the inventory reports with the books of accounts. c.  Require the designated Property Officer to maintain property cards and update them periodically to aid in the conduct of physical count and to solve or minimize the perennial problem of unreconciled Accounting and Property records.
observations.managementAction: To complete the inventory count in CY 2019
observations.reason: The members of the Committee have their respective major duties and functions which demand most of their time and attention, thus, they could not focus on the conduct of the inventory count. The inventory count was not completed

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, with the concurrence of the Sangguniang Bayan of Virac, plan, contruct and manage terminal hub for tricycles and public utility vehicles to enhance economic prosperity thus, a possible increase in revenues coming from the collection of taxes, permit fees and other regulatory fees. We also recommended that the Executive Department seek first the authorization from the legislative department before entering into agreement relative to the operation of a transport terminal, if there will be any. We likewise recommended that the Local Government Unit of Virac enjoin the VTC management to suspend the operation of its transport terminal and provide parking areas for the customers of various businesses, health centers and offices operating therein in consonance with the General Welfare clause of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, Members of the Sangguniang Bayan and all concerned municipal officials strictly adhere to COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012 to avoid disallowance in audit. We also recommended that the Legislative Department re-study the ordinance, seek comments from stakeholders and revise the same to come-up with realistic prices of fish sold in the Municipal jurisdiction and acceptable to all so that the efforts and expenditures for such legislation will not result to wastage of government funds.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive expedite the hiring of new employees and fill-up the vacant position in the LGU funded under the CY 2017 Annual Budget in accordance with the Civil Service Commission rules and regulations so that it could improve its services and programs to the public and provide equal job opportunities to the community.
observations.reason: No reply from Management

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality cause the immediate closure of the LGUs existing controlled dumpsite and construct a sanitary landfill.
observations.reason: Closure of Dumpsite was completed; Rehabilitation was already started and expected to be finished by August 2019

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the LDRRM officer and other concerned agency officials to be decisive in the project implementation, and regularly monitor and evaluate the implementation of programs/projects/activities in compliance with Rule 4, Section 1 of the IRR of RA 10121 to ensure that preparedness, mitigation and rehabilitation projects are implemented in the corresponding budget year to avoid, lessen or limit the potential adverse impact of disasters.

We recommended that the Chairman, Co-Chairman and the members of the Local School Board observe the timeframe in the implementation of SEF programs and projects, and scrutinize and review the details of the proposed SEF Budget taking into consideration the provisions of DepEd, DBM, and DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s 2017, otherwise known as the Revised Guidelines on the Use of the Special Education Fund. We also recommended that the implementation of the CY 2017 SEF Programs/ Projects be fast tracked so that benefits therefrom could be realized in the earliest possible time.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to require the submission of Results of Test Analysis or a Status of Quality Control Test for the construction materials used particularly G.I. sheets, reinforcing steel bars and concrete blocks otherwise related transactions may be suspended in audit. Likewise, require the Municipal Engineer to include in the project inspection test of materials used in the infrastructure projects to ensure project quality. Henceforth, require the Municipal Accountant not to pass in pre-audit or process transactions particularly during final payments Disbursement Vouchers on infrastructure projects which are not supported with Results of Test Analysis from DPWH-BRS/S\DOST/DTI accredited testing laboratories for construction materials, in order to ensure the quality of the construction materials used for the safety of the general public.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the policies on the hiring by the LGU of Virac of contract of service and job order employees have to be revisited, or if there is none, have to be formulated,” to ensure compliance to existing laws, rules and regulations. The Management particularly the Human Resource Officer must comply with the provision of Section 517 of Government Accounting and Auditing Manual and Section 77 of the Local Government Code of 1991.

After determining the fund balances, consider the following courses of actions: 1. Revert the unobligated/unexpended appropriation of projects for re-appropriation; 2. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that: 1) Whenever there seems to be a long period of disagreements among legislators in the review and deliberation of the proposed budget that will hinder the enactment and approval of the Annual Budget, conduct an Executive and Legislative conference to clarify issues and find middle grounds on certain matters which cannot be agreed upon; 2) All Heads implementing departments of the LGU are reminded of the procurement processes and regulations in the implementation of projects. Require Department Heads whose Offices are in charge of the implementation of the specific projects under the 20% EDF to initiate the procurement processes the earliest time possible like on the first day of the January for the projects to be implemented for the first semester, and on the first day of July for the projects to be implemented for the second semester of the year specially for the infrastructure projects with contract period of more than 60 days to ensure 100% project implementation of the annual 20% EDF programs; and 3) Implementation of the CY 2016 projects is fast tracked in the current year so that benefits therefrom could be realized soon.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Local School Board to convene and prepare a comprehensive annual supplemental budget for the operation and maintenance of public schools within the municipality with specific appropriations not lump-sum giving priority to the: 1.) construction, repair, and maintenance of school buildings and other facilities; 2) establishment and maintenance of extension classes; and 3.) sports activities. We also recommended that the Local School Board, composed of the Municipal Mayor as Chairman, the District Supervisor of schools as Co-Chairman, and the Chairman of the Education Committee of the Sangguniang Bayan, the Municipal Treasurer, the President of the Municipal Federation of the Parent-Teacher Association, the elected Representative of Teacher’s Organization and the elected Representative of non-academic personnel of public schools as members, to observe strictly the provision of Sections 99 & 100 of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive consider designating/appointing a permanent Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer who will run/manage the affairs of the office mainly, thus there will be concentration/ focus in the implementation of the disaster programs of the LDRRMC. books first as inventory by the Municipal Accountant and later charged to expense upon
issuance to end users following the procedure set forth under Section 121 of NGAS Vol.
L

We recommended that appropriate disclosures be made in the Notes to
Financial Statements, duly supported by the “List of Not Yet Due and Demandable
Obligations”, along with all valid commitments of the LGU of Virac, Catanduanes for
purchase requisitions on projects/activities already bidded out but the delivery of goods
or services were not yet made in compliance with Section 3.5 of Accounting Circular
Letter No. 2007-002 dated January 19, 2007.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive direct the concerned agency officials to cause the return of the unexpended balance of the Priority ~ Assistance  Fund (PDAF) to the National Government through the Bureau of Treasury.

We recommended that the Head of the Agency require the Solid Waste Management Board, the Municipal Planning Office and all the Departments involve in planning and conceptualizing the Solid Waste Management Plan to give their full attention and cooperation in the revision and finalization of the draft Solid Waste Management Plan of the LGU to be able to comply with the requirement of the National Solid Waste Management Commission.

We likewise recommended that the Local Chief Executive appoint a permanent employee who will be in-charge in the operation of the solid waste management board.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive secure authority from the Sangguniang Bayan of the Local Government Unit of Virac before any local funds are placed under time deposits, and determine whether the same are idle funds or in excess of the normal operating requirement of the LGU, pursuant to Section 21 and 22 of COA Circular No. 92-382.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Engineer to consider revising the program of works for road concreting projects particularly the item for concrete pavement thickness from .15 meter to .20 meter.

‘We recommended the following courses of action to the Management:1.) Compliance to Section 89 of PD NO. 1445 and COA Circular  97-002; 2) Demand for the immediate liquidation from those who have already transferred, retired or separated from government service and request for write off for those employees who are already deceased.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to maintain, update and reconcile cashbooks with accounting records as required under Sections 26, 27 and 28 of the Manual of New Government Accounting System for LGUs, Volume III.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant continue to conduct reconciliation of balances of accounts against relevant records, and effect appropriate adjustments on the deficiencies noted and errors uncovered to reflect the correct balances. To facilitate reconciliation, he should take account of payables with vouchers and complete attachments and write off those payables with no submitted claims.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to observe immediately  the  Internal Control Measure for Cash and COA Circular  92-389 by ensuring that all Disbursement Vouchers and its supporting documents are conspicuously cancelled/invalidated as “PAID” after each payment.
